lede: A look at January 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Oldo Hlaváček and Florian Thauvin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Sikorsky S-76
events.desc: A Sikorsky S-76B flying from John Wayne Airport to Camarillo Airport crashes in Calabasas, 30 miles west of Los Angeles, killing all nine people on board, including five-time NBA champion Kobe Bryant and his daughter Gianna Bryant.

events.title: Antananarivo
events.desc: Rioting breaks out in Antananarivo, Madagascar, sparking a political crisis that will result in the replacement of President Marc Ravalomanana with Andry Rajoelina.

events.title: Turkish Airlines Flight 301
events.desc: Turkish Airlines Flight 301 crashes during takeoff from Izmir Cumaovası Airport (now İzmir Adnan Menderes Airport), killing 66 of the 73 people on board the Fokker F28 Fellowship.

events.title: JAT Flight 367
events.desc: JAT Flight 367 is destroyed by a terrorist bomb, killing 27 of the 28 people on board the DC-9. Flight attendant Vesna Vulović survives with critical injuries.

events.title: Disappearance of the Beaumont children
events.desc: The three Beaumont children disappear from a beach in Glenelg, South Australia, resulting in one of the country's largest-ever police investigations.

events.title: Cairo fire
events.desc: Black Saturday in Egypt: rioters burn Cairo's central business district, targeting British and upper-class Egyptian businesses.

events.title: Hale Telescope
events.desc: The Hale Telescope at Palomar Observatory sees first light under the direction of Edwin Hubble, becoming the largest aperture optical telescope (until BTA-6 is built in 1976).

events.title: Audie Murphy
events.desc: World War II: Audie Murphy displays valor and bravery in action for which he will later be awarded the Medal of Honor.

events.title: Apollo Theater
events.desc: The Apollo Theater reopens in Harlem, New York City.

events.title: Gordon Bremer
events.desc: Gordon Bremer takes formal possession of Hong Kong Island at what is now Possession Point, establishing British Hong Kong.

births.name: Paul Newman
births.line: American actor, activist, director, race car driver, and businessman, co-founded Newman's Own

births.name: Jörg Haider
births.line: Austrian lawyer and politician, Governor of Carinthia

births.name: Anders Fogh Rasmussen
births.line: Danish politician and diplomat, 39th Prime Minister of Denmark

deaths.text: Don Budge, American tennis player and coach
Nelson Rockefeller, American businessman and politician, 41st Vice President of the United States
Dietrich von Hildebrand, German Catholic philosopher and author
Edward Jenner, English physician and immunologist, creator of the smallpox vaccine
Johann Christoph Friedrich Bach, German harpsichord player and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Saint Alberic of Cîteaux
Christian feast day: Saint Paula of Rome
